---
title: "FINAL_18S_Taxonomy"
author: "Nicole Pittoors"
date: "2025-09-05"
output: html_document
---
Load Libraries

```{r, load libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(vegan)
```

# Set working directory

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "Set/Path")

# Also set working directory for interactive use
setwd("Set/Path")

# Set output directory
output_dir <- "./Taxa_Output_18S"

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Verify it was created
cat("Output directory set to:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("Full path:", normalizePath(output_dir), "\n")

```


# Import data
```{r import data}
# Load ASV proportions table, 4th root transformed, no ASVS with zero reads across all samples, ordered west to east and then by sample type, rows are SHIDs, matched metadata
load("./biotic_percs_18S_4th.Rdata")
# ASV raw count table, no ASVs with zero reads across all samples
load("./biotic_clean_18S.Rdata")
# Metadata
load("./metadata_18S.Rdata")
# Metazoan taxonomy table
metazoo_tax_tab0 <- read.delim("./taxa_metazoan_18S.tsv", row.names=1) 
dim(metazoo_tax_tab0) # 7089 x 163

# Remove any ASVs in taxa table that don't match rownames of biotic_percs_18th_4th
metazoo_tax_tab <- metazoo_tax_tab0[match(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_4th), rownames(metazoo_tax_tab0)), ]
dim(metazoo_tax_tab) #6332 x 24
# Extract only the taxonomic hierarchy columns from taxa table
taxonomic_columns <- c("phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")
metazoo_tax_tab <- as.matrix(metazoo_tax_tab[, taxonomic_columns])

```

# Create Taxonomy Phyloseq object
```{r create taxonomy phyloseq object}
# Create phyloseq object with this basic sample data
physeq <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(biotic_percs_18S_4th, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
  sample_data(metadata_18S),
  tax_table(metazoo_tax_tab)
)

# Check if that worked
str(physeq)

# Save
```

# Calculate ASV abundances
Calculate the maximum abundance of each ASV across all samples and then see how many ASVs have a maximum abundance greater than 10% in any sample
Ideal when looking at lower level taxa than just Phyla
```{r ASV abundances}
# Calculate ASV abundances
#ASV_Abundance <- rowSums(biotic_clean_18S)

# Calculate relative abundances by sample (column)
# Convert counts to percentages within each sample
#sample_totals <- colSums(otu_matrix)
#metazo_tpercs <- t(apply(otu_matrix, 1, function(x) x*100/sample_totals))

# Step 3: Find the maximum percentage for each ASV across all samples
#asv_max_percent <- apply(metazo_tpercs, 1, max)

# Step 4: Count how many ASVs have a maximum abundance > 10%
#sum(asv_max_percent > 10) # 49

# You can also see which ASVs these are
#dominant_asvs <- names(asv_max_percent[asv_max_percent > 10])
#head(dominant_asvs) 

# And get their taxonomic information
#tax_info_dominant <- tax_table(physeq)[dominant_asvs, ]
#head(tax_info_dominant)

#-------- Above code but working with already imported data sets
#Convert proportions to percentages 
biotic_percs_18S_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_4th *100

# Find max percentages per ASV across samples
asv_max_percent <- apply(biotic_percs_18S_4th, 1, max)

# Count how many ASVs have a maximum abundance > 10%
sum(asv_max_percent > 10)

# You can also see which ASVs these are
dominant_asvs <- names(asv_max_percent[asv_max_percent > 10])
head(dominant_asvs) 

# And get their taxonomic information
tax_info_dominant <- tax_table(physeq)[dominant_asvs, ]
head(tax_info_dominant)
```
==========================================================
--------------RELATIVE ABUNDANCE PLOTS-------------------
==========================================================

# Custom sample order based on turbidity values
```{r custom sample order}
custom_order_turb <- c(
   # MCG
  "16_MCG_100", "17_MCG_100", "18_MCG_100",
  "16_MCG_500", "17_MCG_500", "18_MCG_500",
  "16_MCG_SESSILE", "17b_MCG_SESSILE", "18_MCG_SESSILE",
  
  # EFGshal
  "31_EFGshal_100", "32_EFGshal_100", "33_EFGshal_100",
  "31_EFGshal_500", "32_EFGshal_500", "33_EFGshal_500",
  "31_EFGshal_SESSILE", "32_EFGshal_SESSILE", "33_EFGshal_SESSILE",
 
   # ALDshal
  "13_ALDshal_100", "14_ALDshal_100", "15_ALDshal_100",
  "13_ALDshal_500", "14_ALDshal_500", "15_ALDshal_500",
  "13_ALDshal_SESSILE", "14_ALDshal_SESSILE", "15_ALDshal_SESSILE",
 
   # BRIshal
  "25_BRIshal_100", "26_BRIshal_100", "26c_BRIshal_100", "27_BRIshal_100",
  "25_BRIshal_500", "26_BRIshal_500", "26b_BRIshal_500", "27_BRIshal_500",
  "25_BRIshal_SESSILE", "26_BRIshal_SESSILE", "27_BRIshal_SESSILE",
  
  # DIAback
  "01_DIAback_100", "02_DIAback_100", "03_DIAback_100",
  "01_DIAback_500", "02_DIAback_500", "03_DIAback_500",
  "01_DIAback_SESSILE", "02_DIAback_SESSILE", "03_DIAback_SESSILE",
  
   # EFGdeep
  "34_EFGdeep_100", "35_EFGdeep_100", "36_EFGdeep_100",
  "34_EFGdeep_500", "35_EFGdeep_500", "36_EFGdeep_500",
  "34_EFGdeep_SESSILE", "35_EFGdeep_SESSILE", "36_EFGdeep_SESSILE",
  
  # DIAcoral
  "04_DIAcoral_100", "05_DIAcoral_100", "06_DIAcoral_100",
  "04_DIAcoral_500", "05_DIAcoral_500", "06_DIAcoral_500",
  "04_DIAcoral_SESSILE", "05_DIAcoral_SESSILE", "06_DIAcoral_SESSILE",
  
  # BRIback
  "22_BRIback_100", "23_BRIback_100", "24_BRIback_100",
  "22_BRIback_500", "23_BRIback_500", "24_BRIback_500",
  "22_BRIback_SESSILE", "23_BRIback_SESSILE", "24_BRIback_SESSILE",
  
  # BRIcoral
  "19_BRIcoral_100", "20_BRIcoral_100", "20b_BRIcoral_100", "21_BRIcoral_100",
  "19_BRIcoral_500", "20_BRIcoral_500", "21_BRIcoral_500",
  "19_BRIcoral_SESSILE", "20_BRIcoral_SESSILE", "21_BRIcoral_SESSILE",
  
   # ALDcoral
  "07_ALDcoral_100", "08_ALDcoral_100", "09_ALDcoral_100",
  "07_ALDcoral_500", "08_ALDcoral_500", "09_ALDcoral_500",
  "07_ALDcoral_SESSILE", "08_ALDcoral_SESSILE", "09_ALDcoral_SESSILE",
  
  # STE
  "28_STE_100", "29_STE_100", "29b_STE_100", "30_STE_100", "30b_STE_100",
  "28_STE_500", "29_STE_500", "30_STE_500", "30b_STE_500",
  "28b_STE_SESSILE", "29b_STE_SESSILE", "30_STE_SESSILE",
  
  # ALDback
  "10_ALDback_100", "11_ALDback_100", "12_ALDback_100",
  "10_ALDback_500", "11_ALDback_500", "12_ALDback_500",
  "10_ALDback_SESSILE", "11_ALDback_SESSILE", "12_ALDback_SESSILE"
)
```


# Create color palette that matches pre-taxa figure
```{r color palette}
c20 <- c("#60714E","#015951","#3B8C6E","#B0DBBB","#5BBFB0","#9DC9D3","#408BA8","#0D3D59","#594A9E","#A8B1D6","#812A5A","#F69B9C","#F16363","#C74238","#FEE14A","#FEBC2C","#F68C30","#F05522","#FDCA9D","grey40")


# Create Phyla mapped palette USE THIS ONE
phylum_color_map <- c("Annelida" = "#60714E",
                      "Chaetognatha" = "#015951",
                      "Arthropoda" = "#3B8C6E",
                      "Sipuncula" = "#B0DBBB",
                      "Bryozoa" = "#5BBFB0",
                      "Brachiopoda" = "#9DC9D3",
                      "Chordata" = "#408BA8",
                      "Rotifera" = "#0D3D59",
                      "Cnidaria" = "#594A9E",
                      "Echinodermata" = "#A8B1D6",
                      "Entoprocta" = "#812A5A",
                      "Mollusca" = "#F69B9C",
                      "Rhodophyta" = "#F16363",
                      "Nematoda" = "#C74238",
                      "Nemertea" = "#FEE14A",
                      "Platyhelminthes" = "#FEBC2C",
                      "Phoronida" = "#C47B51",
                      "Porifera" = "#F68C30",
                      "Ctenophora" = "#F05522",
                      "Gastrotricha" = "#FDCA9D",
                      "Acanthocephala" = "#104820",
                      "Xenacoelomorpha" = "#C47B51",
                      "Unknown" = "grey40")
```

# Create outut directory
```{r output directory}
dir.create("./Taxa_Output_18S", showWarnings = FALSE)

```


# Relative Abundance Plot by SAMPLE
```{r rel. abund. plot by SAMPLE}
# Start with the phyloseq object
otu_matrix <- as(otu_table(physeq), "matrix")
tax_matrix <- as(tax_table(physeq), "matrix")
sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq))

# First, create a data frame with the sample metadata we need
sample_metadata <- data.frame(
  Sample = rownames(sample_data_df),
  sample_id_r = as.character(sample_data_df$sample_id_r),  # Convert to character to avoid list issues
  fraction = gsub(".*_(\\d+|SESSILE)$", "\\1", as.character(sample_data_df$sample_id_r)),
  sitelocality = as.character(sample_data_df$sitelocality),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Calculate phylum-level abundances directly
# First, group ASVs by phylum
phylum_groups <- split(rownames(tax_matrix), tax_matrix[, "phylum"])

# For each phylum, sum the abundances of its ASVs for each sample
phylum_abundances <- lapply(names(phylum_groups), function(phylum) {
  asvs <- phylum_groups[[phylum]]
  if (length(asvs) == 1) {
    # If only one ASV in phylum, just get its abundance
    abundance <- otu_matrix[asvs, , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    # Otherwise sum the abundances of all ASVs in the phylum
    abundance <- colSums(otu_matrix[asvs, , drop = FALSE])
  }
  data.frame(
    Sample = colnames(otu_matrix),
    phylum = phylum,
    Abundance = as.numeric(abundance),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

# Combine all phylum abundances
phylum_abundance_df <- do.call(rbind, phylum_abundances)

# Merge with sample metadata
phylum_abundance_df <- merge(phylum_abundance_df, sample_metadata, by = "Sample")

# Calculate total abundance by phylum
phylum_totals <- aggregate(Abundance ~ phylum, data = phylum_abundance_df, sum)
phylum_totals <- phylum_totals[order(phylum_totals$Abundance, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Get top 20 phyla
top_20_phyla <- phylum_totals$phylum[1:20]


# Filter to only include top 20 phyla
phylum_abundance_df_filtered <- phylum_abundance_df[phylum_abundance_df$phylum %in% top_20_phyla, ]

# Calculate relative abundance
library(dplyr)
relative_data <- phylum_abundance_df_filtered %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Relative_Abundance = Abundance / sum(Abundance) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Order samples by custom order
relative_data$sample_id_r <- factor(relative_data$sample_id_r, levels = custom_order_turb)

# Create variable to label each sample by sitelocality value
# First create a named vector mapping sample_id_r to site
site_labels <- setNames(metadata_ARMS$sitelocality, metadata_ARMS$sample_id_r)
# Remove duplicates if any
site_labels <- site_labels[!duplicated(names(site_labels))]

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(relative_data, aes(x = sample_id_r, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", 
           color = "black", size = 0.2, width = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(~ fraction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labels) +  # This maps sample_id_r to site labels
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "Site", title = "Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 17, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, b = 5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm")
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave("./18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_bySample.pdf", p, width = 18, height = 8)
```

# Relative Abundance Plot - Samples aggregated by SITELOCALITY
This code includes some unclassified samples (maybe phylum is missing?)
```{r Relative Abundance Plot - Samples aggregated by SITELOCALITY}
# Plot top 20 phyla by sitelocality, ordered by turbidity

# Aggregate the data by site locality and fraction
site_aggregated <- relative_data %>%
  group_by(sitelocality, fraction, phylum) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = mean(Relative_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create a sensible ordering for site localities
siteloc_order_turb <- c("McGrail_deep","EFGB_shallow","Alderdice_shallow","Bright_shallow",
                        "Diaphus_deep2", "EFGB_deep", "Diaphus_deep1","Bright_deep2",
                        "Bright_deep1", "Alderdice_deep1", "Stetson_shallow","Alderdice_deep2")

# Set factor order
site_aggregated$sitelocality <- factor(site_aggregated$sitelocality, levels = siteloc_order_turb)

# Create the site-level plot
p_site <- ggplot(site_aggregated, aes(x = sitelocality, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack",
           color = "black", size = 0.4, width = 0.85) +
  facet_grid(~ fraction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "Site Locality", title = "Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla by Site- 18S") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 17, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, b = 5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave("./18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_BySite.pdf", p_site, width = 14, height = 10)
```

Plot top 20 phyla by sitelocality, ordered by turbidity
```{r Plot top 20 phyla by sitelocality, ordered by turbidity}
# Plot top 20 phyla by sitelocality, ordered by turbidity

# Step 1: Check initial data
cat("Initial phyla in data:", 
    length(unique(phylum_abundance_df$phylum)), "\n")
print(unique(phylum_abundance_df$phylum))

# Step 2: Filter to only include top 20 phyla
phylum_abundance_df_filtered <- phylum_abundance_df[phylum_abundance_df$phylum %in% top_20_phyla, ]
cat("\nAfter filtering to top 20:", 
    length(unique(phylum_abundance_df_filtered$phylum)), "\n")

# Step 3: Remove unclassified
phylum_abundance_df_filtered <- phylum_abundance_df_filtered %>%
  filter(!is.na(phylum),
         phylum != "",
         phylum != "Unclassified",
         phylum != "unclassified")

cat("After removing unclassified:", 
    length(unique(phylum_abundance_df_filtered$phylum)), "\n")
print(sort(unique(phylum_abundance_df_filtered$phylum)))

# Step 4: Calculate relative abundance
relative_data <- phylum_abundance_df_filtered %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Relative_Abundance = Abundance / sum(Abundance) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Continue with rest of code...
```

# See top 10 phyla in each fraction
```{r top 10 phyla in each fraction}
# Check phylum abundance by fraction
phylum_by_fraction <- aggregate(Relative_Abundance ~ phylum + fraction, 
                                data = relative_data, 
                                FUN = mean)

# View top phyla by fraction
for(frac in unique(phylum_by_fraction$fraction)) {
  cat("\nTop phyla in fraction", frac, ":\n")
  print(phylum_by_fraction[phylum_by_fraction$fraction == frac, ] %>%
          arrange(desc(Relative_Abundance)) %>%
          head(10))
}
```

===============================================================================
----------- Relative Abundance Table including UNCLASSIFIED READS--------------
===============================================================================
Creating a new ASV count table and phyloseq object to plot relative abundances including unclassified reads

# Load missing ASVs, original AVV count table, and original taxonomy table
```{r rel abund plot with unclassified reads}
# Load missing ASVs (those with no taxonomy hits)
missing_asvs_raw <- read.delim("./missing_asvs.tsv", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
cat("Missing ASVs file dimensions:", dim(missing_asvs_raw), "\n")
cat("First few entries:\n")
print(head(missing_asvs_raw))

# Extract just the ASV IDs (should be in first column)
missing_asv_ids <- missing_asvs_raw$V1
cat("\nTotal missing ASVs:", length(missing_asv_ids), "\n")

# Load original count table
original_counts <- read.csv('/Users/nicolepittoors/Documents/Documents - biscaya/Bioinformatics/ARMS_18S_LULU/18S_lulu_ASVtab.csv', 
                            row.names = 1)
cat("\nOriginal count table dimensions:", dim(original_counts), "\n")
cat("First few row names:", head(rownames(original_counts)), "\n")
cat("First few column names:", head(colnames(original_counts)), "\n")

# Load the COMPLETE taxonomy file (metazoan + non-metazoan)
complete_taxonomy <- read.delim('./final_taxonomy_results.tsv',
                                row.names = 1)

cat("=== Complete Taxonomy File ===\n")
cat("Dimensions:", dim(complete_taxonomy), "\n")
cat("Column names:", colnames(complete_taxonomy), "\n\n")

# Check kingdoms present
if("kingdom" %in% colnames(complete_taxonomy)) {
  cat("Kingdoms present:\n")
  print(table(complete_taxonomy$kingdom))
  cat("\n")
}

# Check phyla
if("phylum" %in% colnames(complete_taxonomy)) {
  cat("Top 20 phyla:\n")
  print(head(sort(table(complete_taxonomy$phylum), decreasing = TRUE), 20))
  cat("\n")
}
```

# Check which missing ASVs are in filtered data
```{r find missing ASVs}
cat("=== ASV Accounting ===\n")
cat("Original count table:", nrow(original_counts), "ASVs\n")
cat("Complete taxonomy file:", nrow(complete_taxonomy), "ASVs\n")
cat("Missing ASVs (no hits):", length(missing_asv_ids), "ASVs\n")

# Calculate non-metazoan
asvs_in_complete_tax <- rownames(complete_taxonomy)
non_metazoan_count <- length(setdiff(asvs_in_complete_tax, rownames(metazoo_tax_tab)))

cat("\nBreakdown:\n")
cat("  Metazoan/Rhodophyta:", nrow(metazoo_tax_tab), "ASVs\n")
cat("  Non-metazoan:", non_metazoan_count, "ASVs\n")
cat("  No taxonomy hits:", length(missing_asv_ids), "ASVs\n")
cat("  Total accounted:", nrow(metazoo_tax_tab) + non_metazoan_count + length(missing_asv_ids), "\n")

# The key question: Which missing ASVs are in ARMS samples?
cat("\n=== Missing ASVs in ARMS Samples ===\n")

# Extract SH IDs from original column names
sh_pattern <- "SH[0-9]+"
original_sh_ids <- regmatches(colnames(original_counts), 
                              regexpr(sh_pattern, colnames(original_counts)))

# Find which columns match your ARMS samples
arms_sample_ids <- colnames(biotic_clean_18S)
arms_cols_in_original <- which(original_sh_ids %in% arms_sample_ids)

cat("Columns in original matching ARMS samples:", length(arms_cols_in_original), "\n")

# Subset to ARMS samples only
original_arms_only <- original_counts[, arms_cols_in_original]
cat("Original table subset to ARMS:", dim(original_arms_only), "\n")

# Check missing ASVs in ARMS samples
missing_in_arms <- intersect(missing_asv_ids, rownames(original_arms_only))
cat("\nMissing ASVs present in original ARMS samples:", length(missing_in_arms), "\n")

if(length(missing_in_arms) > 0) {
  # Calculate their abundances
  missing_arms_abundances <- rowSums(original_arms_only[missing_in_arms, ])
  
  cat("Abundance summary for missing ASVs in ARMS:\n")
  print(summary(missing_arms_abundances))
  
  # How many have non-zero counts?
  missing_with_counts <- sum(missing_arms_abundances > 0)
  cat("\nMissing ASVs with >0 counts in ARMS:", missing_with_counts, "\n")
  cat("Missing ASVs with 0 counts in ARMS:", sum(missing_arms_abundances == 0), "\n")
  
  # These are your "Unclassified" candidates!
  if(missing_with_counts > 0) {
    unclassified_asvs <- names(missing_arms_abundances[missing_arms_abundances > 0])
    cat("\n✓ Found", length(unclassified_asvs), "unclassified ASVs with counts in ARMS samples!\n")
    
    # What percentage of total reads?
    total_reads_all <- sum(original_arms_only)
    unclassified_reads <- sum(missing_arms_abundances[missing_arms_abundances > 0])
    cat("Unclassified reads:", unclassified_reads, 
        "(", round(unclassified_reads/total_reads_all*100, 2), "% of total)\n")
  }
}
```

```{r debugging}
cat("Debugging column name mismatch:\n\n")

# Check what we have
cat("Sample of original_arms_only column names:\n")
print(head(colnames(original_arms_only)))

cat("\nSample of biotic_clean_18S column names:\n")
print(head(colnames(biotic_clean_18S)))

cat("\nSample of original_sh_ids (extracted SH IDs):\n")
print(head(original_sh_ids[arms_cols_in_original]))

cat("\nSample of arms_sample_ids:\n")
print(head(arms_sample_ids))

# The issue is likely the matching - let's fix it
# Extract SH IDs more carefully
original_arms_sh <- original_sh_ids[arms_cols_in_original]

cat("\nChecking if SH IDs are in the same order:\n")
cat("First few from original:", head(original_arms_sh), "\n")
cat("First few from biotic_clean:", head(arms_sample_ids), "\n")

# Try direct assignment if they're in the same order
if(length(original_arms_sh) == length(arms_sample_ids)) {
  cat("\nSame length - checking if same samples in same order...\n")
  cat("Proportion matching:", mean(original_arms_sh == arms_sample_ids), "\n")
  
  if(mean(original_arms_sh == arms_sample_ids) > 0.9) {
    # They're in the same order, just assign directly
    cat("✓ Samples are in same order, assigning directly\n")
    colnames(original_arms_only) <- colnames(biotic_clean_18S)
    colnames(unclassified_counts) <- colnames(biotic_clean_18S)
  } else {
    # They're not in the same order, need to reorder
    cat("✗ Samples are NOT in same order, reordering...\n")
    
    # Create a mapping
    mapping <- data.frame(
      original_col = colnames(original_arms_only),
      sh_id = original_arms_sh
    )
    
    # Match to biotic_clean order
    match_indices <- match(colnames(biotic_clean_18S), mapping$sh_id)
    
    if(any(is.na(match_indices))) {
      cat("ERROR: Some samples in biotic_clean_18S not found in original_arms_only\n")
      cat("Missing samples:", sum(is.na(match_indices)), "\n")
    } else {
      # Reorder columns
      original_arms_only <- original_arms_only[, match_indices]
      unclassified_counts <- unclassified_counts[, match_indices]
      
      # Now assign column names
      colnames(original_arms_only) <- colnames(biotic_clean_18S)
      colnames(unclassified_counts) <- colnames(biotic_clean_18S)
      
      cat("✓ Reordered and column names assigned\n")
    }
  }
} else {
  cat("ERROR: Different number of samples!\n")
  cat("Original ARMS:", length(original_arms_sh), "\n")
  cat("Biotic clean:", length(arms_sample_ids), "\n")
}

# Verify the fix
cat("\nVerification:\n")
cat("Column names now match?", identical(colnames(unclassified_counts), colnames(biotic_clean_18S)), "\n")

if(identical(colnames(unclassified_counts), colnames(biotic_clean_18S))) {
  cat("✓ SUCCESS - proceeding with combining count tables\n\n")
  
  # Now continue with the rest
  cat("Step 2: Combine classified + unclassified counts\n")
  biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown <- rbind(biotic_clean_18S, unclassified_counts)
  cat("  Original biotic_clean_18S:", dim(biotic_clean_18S), "\n")
  cat("  With unclassified added:", dim(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown), "\n\n")
  
  # Step 3: Create taxonomy table for unclassified
  cat("Step 3: Create taxonomy for unclassified ASVs\n")
  unclassified_tax <- matrix(
    "Unclassified",
    nrow = length(unclassified_asvs),
    ncol = 6,
    dimnames = list(unclassified_asvs, c("phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species"))
  )
  
  cat("  Unclassified taxonomy table:", dim(unclassified_tax), "\n\n")
  
  # Step 4: Combine taxonomy tables
  cat("Step 4: Combine taxonomy tables\n")
  metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown <- rbind(metazoo_tax_tab, unclassified_tax)
  cat("  Original metazoo_tax_tab:", dim(metazoo_tax_tab), "\n")
  cat("  With unclassified added:", dim(metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown), "\n\n")
  
  # Step 5: Verify alignment
  cat("Step 5: Verify alignment\n")
  cat("  Row names match?", identical(rownames(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown), 
                                      rownames(metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown)), "\n")
  
  # If not, fix it
  if(!identical(rownames(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown), rownames(metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown))) {
    cat("  Reordering to match...\n")
    metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown <- metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown[rownames(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown), ]
    cat("  After reordering, match?", identical(rownames(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown), 
                                                 rownames(metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown)), "\n")
  }
  
  
  # Step 6: Create new phyloseq object
  cat("Step 6: Create phyloseq object\n")
  physeq18S_w_unknown <- phyloseq(
    otu_table(biotic_clean_18S_w_unknown, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
    sample_data(metadata_18S),
    tax_table(metazoo_tax_tab_w_unknown)
  )
  
  print(physeq18S_w_unknown)
  
  # Step 7: Summary statistics
  cat("\n=== Summary ===\n")
  phylum_table <- table(tax_table(physeq18S_w_unknown)[, "phylum"])
  cat("\nPhylum distribution (top 15):\n")
  print(head(sort(phylum_table, decreasing = TRUE), 15))
  
  unclassified_count <- sum(tax_table(physeq18S_w_unknown)[, "phylum"] == "Unclassified")
  total_asvs <- ntaxa(physeq18S_w_unknown)
  
  cat("\nASV counts:\n")
  cat("  Classified (Metazoa/Rhodophyta):", nrow(metazoo_tax_tab), "\n")
  cat("  Unclassified:", unclassified_count, "\n")
  cat("  Total:", total_asvs, "\n")
  cat("  Percentage unclassified:", round(unclassified_count/total_asvs*100, 2), "%\n")
  
  # Check read abundance
  cat("\nRead abundance:\n")
  total_reads <- sum(otu_table(physeq18S_w_unknown))
  unclassified_reads_check <- sum(otu_table(physeq18S_w_unknown)[rownames(unclassified_tax), ])
  cat("  Total reads:", total_reads, "\n")
  cat("  Unclassified reads:", unclassified_reads_check, "\n")
  cat("  Percentage unclassified reads:", round(unclassified_reads_check/total_reads*100, 2), "%\n")
  
} else {
  cat("✗ FAILED - need to investigate further\n")
}
```

# Create relative abundance plots
```{r create plot w/ unclassified reads}
# Use the same custom order and color palette
custom_order_turb <- c(
  # MCG
  "16_MCG_100", "17_MCG_100", "18_MCG_100",
  "16_MCG_500", "17_MCG_500", "18_MCG_500",
  "16_MCG_SESSILE", "17b_MCG_SESSILE", "18_MCG_SESSILE",
  
  # EFGshal
  "31_EFGshal_100", "32_EFGshal_100", "33_EFGshal_100",
  "31_EFGshal_500", "32_EFGshal_500", "33_EFGshal_500",
  "31_EFGshal_SESSILE", "32_EFGshal_SESSILE", "33_EFGshal_SESSILE",
  
  # ALDshal
  "13_ALDshal_100", "14_ALDshal_100", "15_ALDshal_100",
  "13_ALDshal_500", "14_ALDshal_500", "15_ALDshal_500",
  "13_ALDshal_SESSILE", "14_ALDshal_SESSILE", "15_ALDshal_SESSILE",
  
  # BRIshal
  "25_BRIshal_100", "26_BRIshal_100", "26c_BRIshal_100", "27_BRIshal_100",
  "25_BRIshal_500", "26_BRIshal_500", "26b_BRIshal_500", "27_BRIshal_500",
  "25_BRIshal_SESSILE", "26_BRIshal_SESSILE", "27_BRIshal_SESSILE",
  
  # DIAback
  "01_DIAback_100", "02_DIAback_100", "03_DIAback_100",
  "01_DIAback_500", "02_DIAback_500", "03_DIAback_500",
  "01_DIAback_SESSILE", "02_DIAback_SESSILE", "03_DIAback_SESSILE",
  
  # EFGdeep
  "34_EFGdeep_100", "35_EFGdeep_100", "36_EFGdeep_100",
  "34_EFGdeep_500", "35_EFGdeep_500", "36_EFGdeep_500",
  "34_EFGdeep_SESSILE", "35_EFGdeep_SESSILE", "36_EFGdeep_SESSILE",
  
  # DIAcoral
  "04_DIAcoral_100", "05_DIAcoral_100", "06_DIAcoral_100",
  "04_DIAcoral_500", "05_DIAcoral_500", "06_DIAcoral_500",
  "04_DIAcoral_SESSILE", "05_DIAcoral_SESSILE", "06_DIAcoral_SESSILE",
  
  # BRIback
  "22_BRIback_100", "23_BRIback_100", "24_BRIback_100",
  "22_BRIback_500", "23_BRIback_500", "24_BRIback_500",
  "22_BRIback_SESSILE", "23_BRIback_SESSILE", "24_BRIback_SESSILE",
  
  # BRIcoral
  "19_BRIcoral_100", "20_BRIcoral_100", "20b_BRIcoral_100", "21_BRIcoral_100",
  "19_BRIcoral_500", "20_BRIcoral_500", "21_BRIcoral_500",
  "19_BRIcoral_SESSILE", "20_BRIcoral_SESSILE", "21_BRIcoral_SESSILE",
  
  # ALDcoral
  "07_ALDcoral_100", "08_ALDcoral_100", "09_ALDcoral_100",
  "07_ALDcoral_500", "08_ALDcoral_500", "09_ALDcoral_500",
  "07_ALDcoral_SESSILE", "08_ALDcoral_SESSILE", "09_ALDcoral_SESSILE",
  
  # STE
  "28_STE_100", "29_STE_100", "29b_STE_100", "30_STE_100", "30b_STE_100",
  "28_STE_500", "29_STE_500", "30_STE_500", "30b_STE_500",
  "28b_STE_SESSILE", "29b_STE_SESSILE", "30_STE_SESSILE",
  
  # ALDback
  "10_ALDback_100", "11_ALDback_100", "12_ALDback_100",
  "10_ALDback_500", "11_ALDback_500", "12_ALDback_500",
  "10_ALDback_SESSILE", "11_ALDback_SESSILE", "12_ALDback_SESSILE"
)

# Color palette with Unclassified
phylum_color_map_w_unknown <- c(
  "Annelida" = "#60714E",
  "Arthropoda" = "#3B8C6E",
  "Sipuncula" = "#B0DBBB",
  "Bryozoa" = "#5BBFB0",
  "Brachiopoda" = "#9DC9D3",
  "Chordata" = "#408BA8",
  "Rotifera" = "#0D3D59",
  "Cnidaria" = "#594A9E",
  "Echinodermata" = "#A8B1D6",
  "Entoprocta" = "#812A5A",
  "Mollusca" = "#F69B9C",
  "Rhodophyta" = "#F16363",
  "Nematoda" = "#C74238",
  "Nemertea" = "#FEE14A",
  "Platyhelminthes" = "#FEBC2C",
  "Porifera" = "#F68C30",
  "Gastrotricha" = "#FDCA9D",
  "Xenacoelomorpha" = "#C47B51",  
  "Acanthocephala" = "#104820",   
  "Unclassified" = "grey70"
)

# Calculate phylum-level abundances from physeq18S_w_unknown
otu_matrix <- as(otu_table(physeq18S_w_unknown), "matrix")
tax_matrix <- as(tax_table(physeq18S_w_unknown), "matrix")
sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq18S_w_unknown))

# Create sample metadata
sample_metadata <- data.frame(
  Sample = rownames(sample_data_df),
  sample_id_r = as.character(sample_data_df$sample_id_r),
  fraction = gsub(".*_(\\d+|SESSILE)$", "\\1", as.character(sample_data_df$sample_id_r)),
  sitelocality = as.character(sample_data_df$sitelocality),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Group ASVs by phylum
phylum_groups <- split(rownames(tax_matrix), tax_matrix[, "phylum"])

# Calculate phylum abundances
phylum_abundances <- lapply(names(phylum_groups), function(phylum) {
  asvs <- phylum_groups[[phylum]]
  if (length(asvs) == 1) {
    abundance <- otu_matrix[asvs, , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    abundance <- colSums(otu_matrix[asvs, , drop = FALSE])
  }
  data.frame(
    Sample = colnames(otu_matrix),
    phylum = phylum,
    Abundance = as.numeric(abundance),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

phylum_abundance_df <- do.call(rbind, phylum_abundances)
phylum_abundance_df <- merge(phylum_abundance_df, sample_metadata, by = "Sample")

# Get top 20 phyla (which now includes Unclassified)
phylum_totals <- aggregate(Abundance ~ phylum, data = phylum_abundance_df, sum)
phylum_totals <- phylum_totals[order(phylum_totals$Abundance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
top_20_phyla <- phylum_totals$phylum[1:20]

cat("Top 20 phyla (including Unclassified):\n")
print(phylum_totals[1:20, ])

# Filter to top 20
phylum_abundance_df_filtered <- phylum_abundance_df[phylum_abundance_df$phylum %in% top_20_phyla, ]

# Calculate relative abundance
library(dplyr)
relative_data <- phylum_abundance_df_filtered %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Relative_Abundance = Abundance / sum(Abundance) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Order samples
relative_data$sample_id_r <- factor(relative_data$sample_id_r, levels = custom_order_turb)

# Create site labels
site_labels <- setNames(metadata_18S$sitelocality, metadata_18S$sample_id_r)
site_labels <- site_labels[!duplicated(names(site_labels))]

# Create the plot
p_with_unknown <- ggplot(relative_data, aes(x = sample_id_r, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", 
           color = "black", size = 0.2, width = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(~ fraction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map_w_unknown) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labels) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "Site", 
       title = "Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla (Including Unclassified) - 18S") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 17, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, b = 5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "cm")
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_bySample_WITH_UNCLASSIFIED.pdf"), 
       p_with_unknown, width = 18, height = 8)

cat("\n✓ Plot saved to:", file.path(output_dir, "18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_bySample_WITH_UNCLASSIFIED.pdf"), "\n")

# Display the plot
print(p_with_unknown)
```


# Checkinging into 100um fraction plotting as unknown
```{r}
cat("=== Debugging: Which phyla are missing colors? ===\n\n")

# Get all phyla actually in your top 20 plot data
phyla_in_plot <- unique(relative_data$phylum)
cat("Phyla in the plot data (top 20):\n")
print(sort(phyla_in_plot))

# Get phyla in your color palette
phyla_in_palette <- names(phylum_color_map_w_unknown)
cat("\n\nPhyla in color palette:\n")
print(sort(phyla_in_palette))

# Find which phyla are in plot but NOT in palette
missing_colors <- setdiff(phyla_in_plot, phyla_in_palette)
cat("\n\nPhyla in plot but MISSING from color palette:\n")
print(missing_colors)

# Find which phyla are in palette but NOT in plot
extra_colors <- setdiff(phyla_in_palette, phyla_in_plot)
cat("\n\nPhyla in palette but NOT in plot (these are OK):\n")
print(extra_colors)

# Check what ggplot is assigning to missing phyla
if(length(missing_colors) > 0) {
  cat("\n\n⚠️ PROBLEM: These phyla don't have assigned colors!\n")
  cat("ggplot will assign default colors to them.\n")
  cat("This is why you're seeing grey40 (ggplot's default grey)\n\n")
  
  # Show abundance of missing phyla
  for(p in missing_colors) {
    total_abund <- sum(relative_data$Relative_Abundance[relative_data$phylum == p])
    cat(p, "- Total relative abundance:", round(total_abund, 2), "%\n")
  }
}
```

# Relative Abundance Plot - Samples aggregated by SITELOCALITY (with Unclassified Reads)
```{r}
# Aggregate the data by site locality and fraction
site_aggregated <- relative_data %>%
  group_by(sitelocality, fraction, phylum) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = mean(Relative_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create a sensible ordering for site localities (by turbidity)
siteloc_order_turb <- c("McGrail_deep", "EFGB_shallow", "Alderdice_shallow", "Bright_shallow",
                        "Diaphus_deep2", "EFGB_deep", "Diaphus_deep1", "Bright_deep2",
                        "Bright_deep1", "Alderdice_deep1", "Stetson_shallow", "Alderdice_deep2")

# Set factor order
site_aggregated$sitelocality <- factor(site_aggregated$sitelocality, levels = siteloc_order_turb)

# Create the site-level plot with updated palette
p_site_w_unknown <- ggplot(site_aggregated, aes(x = sitelocality, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack",
           color = "black", size = 0.4, width = 0.85) +
  facet_grid(~ fraction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map_w_unknown) +  # Updated palette name
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", x = "Site Locality", 
       title = "Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla by Site (Including Unclassified) - 18S") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 17, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, b = 5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_BySite_WITH_UNCLASSIFIED.pdf"), 
       p_site_w_unknown, width = 14, height = 10)

cat("\n✓ Site-aggregated plot saved to:", 
    file.path(output_dir, "18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_BySite_WITH_UNCLASSIFIED.pdf"), "\n")

# Display the plot
print(p_site_w_unknown)
```





=====================================
----------- ANCOMBC------------------
=====================================
# Load libraries
```{r load ANCOMBC2 libraries}
library(phyloseq)
library(ANCOMBC)
library(tidyverse)
library(conflicted)
library(foreach)
library(rngtools)
library(reshape2)
```


# Create phyloseq object using raw counts
biotic_clean = asv table
metazoo_taxatab rows = ASVs

```{r create phyloseq object using raw counts}
# Make sure ASV table is correct dimensions and a matrix
biotic_clean_18S <- as.matrix(biotic_clean_18S)
str(biotic_clean_18S)

# Create phyloseq object
ancom_physeq <- phyloseq(otu_table(biotic_clean_18S, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
                                sample_data(metadata_18S),
                                tax_table(metazoo_tax_tab))
# Export phyloseq object
     save(ancom_physeq, file = "/Users/nicolepittoors/Documents/Documents - biscaya/Bioinformatics/ARMS_18S_LULU/FINAL_18S_Pipeline/Taxa_Output_18S/ancombc_raw_counts_physeq_18S.RData")
```

#Set prv_cut = 0.1. 0.1667 may be too conservative. 
Use raw count data
Since size fraction is a group, that would be 1/6 of the samples = 0.1667
Using psuedo_sens = TRUE
Pseudo-counts are small values (0.1, 0.5, 1) added to zero counts before log transformation, 
but different pseudo-count choices can lead to false positives. The sensitivity analysis 
(pseudo_sens = TRUE) re-runs the analysis with multiple pseudo-count values to identify 
which significant results are robust across different choices versus those that are artifacts 
of the specific pseudo-count used. This ensures our differentially abundant taxa findings are 
reliable and not dependent on arbitrary data preprocessing decisions.
```{r fraction check ANCOMBC}
fraction_ancombc <- ancombc2(data = ancom_physeq, 
                            fix_formula = "fraction",
                             global = TRUE, 
                             p_adj_method = "BH", 
                             prv_cut = 0.10, 
                             group = "fraction",
                            pseudo_sens = TRUE)

#Check for results
print(fraction_ancombc$res_global)
```


# 1) Exctract top 20 most significant ASVs
Extract the results from the ANCOMBC2 output, sort them by their p-values (or adjusted p-values), and then select the top 20 ASVs
```{r Extract top 20 most significant ASVs}
# Extract results from ANCOMBC2 output
ancom_res <- fraction_ancombc$res_global

# Sort the ASVs by the p-value or adjusted p-value (q_val) and select the top 20
top20_asvs <- ancom_res %>%
  dplyr::arrange(q_val) %>%
  head(20) %>%
  pull(taxon)  # Extract the ASV IDs

# Print the top 20 ASVs
print(top20_asvs)
```


2) Map the ASVs to their scientific name
```{r map ASVs to scientific names}
# Extract the taxonomic table from the phyloseq object
tax_table <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ancom_physeq))

# Filter the taxonomic table to only include the top 20 ASVs
top20_taxonomy <- tax_table[rownames(tax_table) %in% top20_asvs, ]

# Melt the phyloseq object (this already includes taxonomy)
physeq_melted <- psmelt(ancom_physeq)

# Filter to only include the top 20 ASVs 
physeq_melted_top20 <- physeq_melted[physeq_melted$OTU %in% top20_asvs, ]

# Check the result
colnames(physeq_melted_top20)  # Should have clean column names

# Check cleaned column names
colnames(physeq_melted_clean)

# Remove ALL duplicate columns, keeping only the first occurrence
physeq_melted_clean <- physeq_melted_clean[, !duplicated(colnames(physeq_melted_clean))]

# Now run your analysis
physeq_melted_clean <- physeq_melted_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(OTU %in% top20_asvs) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fraction, Sample) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Sample = factor(Sample, levels = unique(Sample)))

colnames(physeq_melted_clean)
```


3) Plot the abundance of the top 20 ASVs
```{r Plot the abundance of the top 20 ASVs}
# Check if 'fraction' column exists
if (!"fraction" %in% colnames(physeq_melted_clean)) {
  stop("The 'fraction' column is not found in the sample data.")
}

# Order the samples by 'fraction' and 'Sample'
physeq_melted_frac <- physeq_melted_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(OTU %in% top20_asvs) %>%  # Filter only top 20 ASVs
  dplyr::mutate(Sample = factor(Sample, levels = unique(Sample[order(fraction, Sample)])))

# Create the plot with custom colors
fraction_plot1 <- ggplot(physeq_melted_frac, aes(x = Sample, y = Abundance, fill = family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Significant ASVs Grouped by Fraction-18S", 
       x = "Sample", 
       y = "Abundance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  facet_grid(~ fraction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c20)  

# Print the plot
print(fraction_plot1)

# Save
ggsave("/Users/nicolepittoors/Documents/Documents - biscaya/Bioinformatics/ARMS_18S_LULU/FINAL_18S_Pipeline/Taxa_Output_18S/ANCOMBC_abun_fraction_18S.pdf",
      plot = last_plot(), 
       device = "pdf", 
       scale = 1, 
       width = 11, 
       height = 8.5, 
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300)
```


# Export .CSV of mapped unique families
```{r export csv of unique families}
tax_subset_18S <- tax_table[, c("phylum", "class", "order", "family")]
unique_families_18S <- unique(tax_subset_18S)

# Remove any rows where Family is NA
unique_families_18S <- unique_families_18S[order(unique_families_18S$phylum, 
                                          unique_families_18S$class,
                                          unique_families_18S$order,
                                          unique_families_18S$family), ]


unique_families_clean_18S <- unique_families_18S[!grepl("[^[:alnum:]]$", unique_families_18S$family), ]

# Remove taxonomic errors
taxonomic_errors <- c("Annelida", "Nemertea", "Phoronida", "Phyllodocida", 
                      "Chromadorea", "Pteropoda")

unique_families_metazoa_only <- unique_families_clean_18S[
  !unique_families_clean_18S$family %in% taxonomic_errors, ]

write.csv(unique_families_metazoa_only, 
          file = "/Users/nicolepittoors/Documents/Documents - biscaya/Bioinformatics/ARMS_18S_LULU/FINAL_18S_Pipeline/Taxa_Output_18S/unique_families_18S_clean.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```


# Subset sessile and motile fractions
```{r subset fractions}
# Subset phyloseq objects - combine 100 and 500 as motile
sessile_physeq <- subset_samples(ancom_physeq, fraction == "sessile")
motile_physeq <- subset_samples(ancom_physeq, fraction %in% c("100", "500")) 

# Check sample sizes
cat("Sessile samples:", nsamples(sessile_physeq), "\n")
cat("Motile samples (100 + 500):", nsamples(motile_physeq), "\n")

# Check the breakdown of motile samples
motile_breakdown <- table(sample_data(motile_physeq)$fraction)
cat("Motile fraction breakdown:\n")
print(motile_breakdown)

# Remove taxa with zero abundance after subsetting
sessile_physeq <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(sessile_physeq) > 0, sessile_physeq)
motile_physeq <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(motile_physeq) > 0, motile_physeq)

cat("\n=== SESSILE FRACTION ANALYSIS ===\n")
sessile_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = sessile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "turbidity_std_rank + depth",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.10
)

cat("\n=== MOTILE FRACTIONS (100 + 500) ANALYSIS ===\n")
motile_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = motile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "turbidity_std_rank + depth",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.10
)

# Extract sessile results
sessile_turb <- sessile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(q_turbidity_std_rank < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(q_turbidity_std_rank) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_turbidity_std_rank, p_turbidity_std_rank, q_turbidity_std_rank)

sessile_depth <- sessile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(q_depth < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(q_depth) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_depth, p_depth, q_depth)

cat("Sessile - Significant phyla for turbidity:\n")
print(sessile_turb)
cat("Sessile - Significant phyla for depth:\n")
print(sessile_depth)

# Extract motile results
motile_turb <- motile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(q_turbidity_std_rank < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(q_turbidity_std_rank) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_turbidity_std_rank, p_turbidity_std_rank, q_turbidity_std_rank)

motile_depth <- motile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(q_depth < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(q_depth) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_depth, p_depth, q_depth)

cat("Motile - Significant phyla for turbidity:\n")
print(motile_turb)
cat("Motile - Significant phyla for depth:\n")
print(motile_depth)
```


# Test for significant differences in Phyla abundances across turbidty and depth gradients in ALL SAMPLES
```{r phlya differences depth/turb all samples}
## CHECK SPECIFICALLY FOR RHODOPHYTA
cat("\n=== RHODOPHYTA SPECIFIC RESULTS ===\n")

# Sessile Rhodophyta
sessile_rhodo <- sessile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon == "Rhodophyta") %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_turbidity_std_rank, q_turbidity_std_rank, lfc_depth, q_depth)

# Motile Rhodophyta  
motile_rhodo <- motile_ancombc$res %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon == "Rhodophyta") %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, lfc_turbidity_std_rank, q_turbidity_std_rank, lfc_depth, q_depth)

cat("Sessile Rhodophyta results:\n")
print(sessile_rhodo)
cat("Motile Rhodophyta results:\n")
print(motile_rhodo)
```



# ----------------------- ANCOM-BC2 FRACTION, SITE, DEPTH + TURB ------------


#  ------ 1. ANCOMBC phyla and families associated with each FRACTION ------
Which taxa are enriched in each fraction
Size-based community structure
```{r fraction effects, phylum fam}
# ========== FRACTION EFFECTS ANALYSIS  ========

# Phylum level - fraction effects
fraction_P_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "fraction",  # Testing 100µm vs 500µm vs sessile
  global = TRUE,             # Global test across fractions
  pairwise = TRUE,           # Which specific fractions differ
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "fraction",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# Family level - fraction effects  
fraction_F_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "fraction",
  global = TRUE,
  pairwise = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "fraction",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# Function to extract and format fraction results
extract_fraction_results <- function(ancombc_result, tax_level) {
  
  # Global results (which taxa differ between fractions)
  global_res <- ancombc_result$res_global
  global_res$Analysis <- paste0("Fraction_Effects_", tax_level)
  global_res$Test_Type <- "Global"
  
  # Pairwise results (which specific fraction pairs differ)
  pairwise_res <- ancombc_result$res_pair
  pairwise_res$Analysis <- paste0("Fraction_Effects_", tax_level)
  pairwise_res$Test_Type <- "Pairwise"
  
  return(list(global = global_res, pairwise = pairwise_res))
}

# Extract fraction results
fraction_phylum_results <- extract_fraction_results(fraction_P_ancombc, "Phylum")
fraction_family_results <- extract_fraction_results(fraction_F_ancombc, "Family")

# Export fraction results
write.csv(fraction_phylum_results$global, file.path(output_dir, "fraction_phylum_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(fraction_phylum_results$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "fraction_phylum_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(fraction_family_results$global, file.path(output_dir, "fraction_family_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(fraction_family_results$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "fraction_family_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```




# ------ 2. ANCOMBC phyla and families associated with each SITE -------

Answers the questions:
Which taxa vary significantly across the 12 sites
Unmeasured environmental factors creating site-specific communities
Geographic patterns in community structure
```{r site, phylum and fam}
# 1. All samples - Phylum level
site_global_P_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",  # Categorical site variable
  global = TRUE,               # Global test across all sites
  pairwise = TRUE,             # Which specific sites differ
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",      # Grouping variable for global test
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 2. All samples - Family level  
site_global_F_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  pairwise = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH", 
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 3. Motile site effects - Phylum
motile_site_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = motile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 4. Motile site effects - Family
motile_site_FAMancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = motile_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 5. Sessile site effects - Phylum
sessile_site_ancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = sessile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 6. Sessile site effects - Family
sessile_site_FAMancombc <- ancombc2(
  data = sessile_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# EXPORT SITE EFFECTS RESULTS

# Function to extract and format site results
extract_site_results <- function(ancombc_result, analysis_name) {
  
  # Global results
  global_res <- ancombc_result$res_global
  global_res$Analysis <- analysis_name
  global_res$Test_Type <- "Global"
  
  # Pairwise results (if available)
  if("res_pair" %in% names(ancombc_result)) {
    pairwise_res <- ancombc_result$res_pair  
    pairwise_res$Analysis <- analysis_name
    pairwise_res$Test_Type <- "Pairwise"
  } else {
    pairwise_res <- data.frame()
  }
  
  return(list(global = global_res, pairwise = pairwise_res))
}

# Extract all site results
site_all_phylum <- extract_site_results(site_global_P_ancombc, "All_Samples_Phylum")
site_all_family <- extract_site_results(site_global_F_ancombc, "All_Samples_Family")
site_motile_phylum <- extract_site_results(motile_site_ancombc, "Motile_Phylum")
site_motile_family <- extract_site_results(motile_site_FAMancombc, "Motile_Family")
site_sessile_phylum <- extract_site_results(sessile_site_ancombc, "Sessile_Phylum")
site_sessile_family <- extract_site_results(sessile_site_FAMancombc, "Sessile_Family")

# Export individual site global results
write.csv(site_all_phylum$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_all_phylum_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_all_family$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_all_family_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_motile_phylum$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_motile_phylum_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_motile_family$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_motile_family_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_sessile_phylum$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_sessile_phylum_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_sessile_family$global, file.path(output_dir, "site_sessile_family_global_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Export individual site pairwise results (if available)
write.csv(site_all_phylum$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_all_phylum_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_all_family$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_all_family_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_motile_phylum$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_motile_phylum_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_motile_family$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_motile_family_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_sessile_phylum$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_sessile_phylum_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_sessile_family$pairwise, file.path(output_dir, "site_sessile_family_pairwise_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Combine all global site results for comparison
all_site_global <- rbind(
  site_all_phylum$global,
  site_all_family$global,
  site_motile_phylum$global,
  site_motile_family$global,
  site_sessile_phylum$global,
  site_sessile_family$global
)

write.csv(all_site_global, file.path(output_dir, "all_site_global_results_combined_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Create summary of significant results
create_summary <- function(global_results, analysis_name) {
  n_significant <- sum(global_results$diff_abn, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_total <- nrow(global_results)
  return(data.frame(
    Analysis = analysis_name,
    Total_Taxa = n_total,
    Significant_Taxa = n_significant,
    Percent_Significant = round((n_significant/n_total)*100, 1)
  ))
}

# Create summary table
summary_table <- rbind(
  create_summary(site_all_phylum$global, "All_Samples_Phylum"),
  create_summary(site_all_family$global, "All_Samples_Family"),
  create_summary(site_motile_phylum$global, "Motile_Phylum"),
  create_summary(site_motile_family$global, "Motile_Family"),
  create_summary(site_sessile_phylum$global, "Sessile_Phylum"),
  create_summary(site_sessile_family$global, "Sessile_Family"),
  create_summary(fraction_phylum_results$global, "Fraction_Effects_Phylum"),
  create_summary(fraction_family_results$global, "Fraction_Effects_Family")
)

write.csv(summary_table, file.path(output_dir, "all_analyses_summary_table_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
print(summary_table)
```


```{r}
# Set preference for dplyr functions
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)

# Get all LFC columns (both individual sites and pairwise comparisons)
lfc_cols <- grep("^lfc_sitelocality", colnames(site_all_phylum$pairwise), value = TRUE)
p_cols <- grep("^p_sitelocality", colnames(site_all_phylum$pairwise), value = TRUE)

# Summary of effect sizes across all comparisons
all_lfc_values <- unlist(site_all_phylum$pairwise[, lfc_cols])
cat("Summary of all log-fold changes across site comparisons:\n")
print(summary(abs(all_lfc_values)))

# Find the largest effects for each taxon
max_effects_per_taxon <- site_all_phylum$pairwise %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    max_lfc = max(abs(c_across(all_of(lfc_cols))), na.rm = TRUE),
    min_pval = min(c_across(all_of(p_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, max_lfc, min_pval) %>%
  arrange(desc(max_lfc))

cat("Taxa with largest site effects:\n")
print(max_effects_per_taxon)

# Count significant raw p-values (before correction)
raw_sig_count <- site_all_phylum$pairwise %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, all_of(p_cols)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    significant_raw_comparisons = sum(c_across(all_of(p_cols)) < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_comparisons = length(p_cols)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, significant_raw_comparisons, total_comparisons) %>%
  arrange(desc(significant_raw_comparisons))

cat("Raw significant comparisons per taxon (before multiple testing correction):\n")
print(raw_sig_count)
```


# ------ 3. ANCOMBC phyla and families associated with DEPTH AND TURB ------
```{r depth and turb, phylum fam}
# ============ ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS ANALYSIS: DEPTH + TURBIDITY ===============
# Testing across All Samples, Motile Fractions, and Sessile Fractions

# Create ARMS ID for random effects (if needed later)
sample_data(ancom_physeq)$ARMS_corrected <- paste(
  sample_data(ancom_physeq)$sitelocality,
  sample_data(ancom_physeq)$ARMS_ID_corrected,
  sep = "_"
)

# ============== 1. ANCOM-BC2 ANALYSES: Environmental Effects (Depth + Turbidity) ==============

cat("Running ANCOM-BC2 environmental analyses...\n")

# 1/6 All Samples - Phylum Level
env_phylum_all <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 2/6 All Samples - Family Level  
env_family_all <- ancombc2(
  data = ancom_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 3/6 Motile Fractions - Phylum Level
env_phylum_motile <- ancombc2(
  data = motile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 4/6 Motile Fractions - Family Level
env_family_motile <- ancombc2(
  data = motile_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 5/6 Sessile Fraction - Phylum Level
env_phylum_sessile <- ancombc2(
  data = sessile_physeq,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# 6/6 Sessile Fraction - Family Level
env_family_sessile <- ancombc2(
  data = sessile_physeq,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "depth + turbidity_std_rank",
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

cat("All ANCOM-BC2 analyses completed.\n")


# ============== 2. EXTRACT RESULTS =============

# Extract results objects
env_results_phylum_all <- env_phylum_all$res
env_results_family_all <- env_family_all$res
env_results_phylum_motile <- env_phylum_motile$res
env_results_family_motile <- env_family_motile$res
env_results_phylum_sessile <- env_phylum_sessile$res
env_results_family_sessile <- env_family_sessile$res

# ============= 3. FUNCTION TO EXTRACT SIGNIFICANT TAXA =========
extract_significant_environmental_taxa <- function(results, analysis_name, fraction_type, tax_level) {
  
  # Extract significant taxa for depth
  depth_sig_indices <- which(results$diff_depth == TRUE)
  turbidity_sig_indices <- which(results$diff_turbidity_std_rank == TRUE)
  
  # Create depth results dataframe
  if(length(depth_sig_indices) > 0) {
    depth_results <- data.frame(
      Analysis = analysis_name,
      Fraction = fraction_type,
      Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
      Environmental_Variable = "Depth",
      Taxon = results$taxon[depth_sig_indices],
      Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_depth[depth_sig_indices], 4),
      P_value = results$p_depth[depth_sig_indices],
      Q_value = results$q_depth[depth_sig_indices],
      Robust = results$passed_ss_depth[depth_sig_indices],
      Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_depth[depth_sig_indices] > 0, "Increases_with_depth", "Decreases_with_depth"),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    depth_results <- data.frame()
  }
  
  # Create turbidity results dataframe
  if(length(turbidity_sig_indices) > 0) {
    turbidity_results <- data.frame(
      Analysis = analysis_name,
      Fraction = fraction_type,
      Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
      Environmental_Variable = "Turbidity",
      Taxon = results$taxon[turbidity_sig_indices],
      Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_sig_indices], 4),
      P_value = results$p_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_sig_indices],
      Q_value = results$q_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_sig_indices],
      Robust = results$passed_ss_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_sig_indices],
      Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_sig_indices] > 0, "Increases_with_turbidity", "Decreases_with_turbidity"),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    turbidity_results <- data.frame()
  }
  
  # Combine and return
  combined_results <- rbind(depth_results, turbidity_results)
  return(combined_results)
}

# ==============  4. EXTRACT SIGNIFICANT RESULTS FOR ALL ANALYSES ================

cat("Extracting significant results...\n")

# Extract significant results
sig_env_phylum_all <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_phylum_all, "Environmental_Effects", "All_Samples", "Phylum")
sig_env_family_all <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_family_all, "Environmental_Effects", "All_Samples", "Family")
sig_env_phylum_motile <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_phylum_motile, "Environmental_Effects", "Motile", "Phylum")
sig_env_family_motile <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_family_motile, "Environmental_Effects", "Motile", "Family")
sig_env_phylum_sessile <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_phylum_sessile, "Environmental_Effects", "Sessile", "Phylum")
sig_env_family_sessile <- extract_significant_environmental_taxa(env_results_family_sessile, "Environmental_Effects", "Sessile", "Family")

# Combine all significant results
all_environmental_significant <- rbind(
  sig_env_phylum_all,
  sig_env_family_all,
  sig_env_phylum_motile,
  sig_env_family_motile,
  sig_env_phylum_sessile,
  sig_env_family_sessile
)

# ===================  5. EXPORT RESULTS TO CSV ===================

cat("Exporting results to CSV files...\n")

# Export full results (all taxa, not just significant)
write.csv(env_results_phylum_all, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_all_samples_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_results_family_all, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_all_samples_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_results_phylum_motile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_motile_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_results_family_motile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_motile_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_results_phylum_sessile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_sessile_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_results_family_sessile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_sessile_full_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Export significant results only
write.csv(sig_env_phylum_all, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_all_samples_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_env_family_all, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_all_samples_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_env_phylum_motile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_motile_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_env_family_motile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_motile_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_env_phylum_sessile, file.path(output_dir,  "environmental_phylum_sessile_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_env_family_sessile, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_sessile_significant_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Export combined significant results
write.csv(all_environmental_significant, file.path(output_dir, "all_environmental_significant_results_18S.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# =================== 6. CREATE SUMMARY TABLE ===================

env_summary_table <- data.frame(
  Analysis = c("All_Samples_Phylum", "All_Samples_Family", "Motile_Phylum", "Motile_Family", "Sessile_Phylum", "Sessile_Family"),
  Total_Taxa_Tested = c(
    nrow(env_results_phylum_all), nrow(env_results_family_all),
    nrow(env_results_phylum_motile), nrow(env_results_family_motile),
    nrow(env_results_phylum_sessile), nrow(env_results_family_sessile)
  ),
  Significant_Depth = c(
    sum(env_results_phylum_all$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_all$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_phylum_motile$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_motile$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_phylum_sessile$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_sessile$diff_depth, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  Significant_Turbidity = c(
    sum(env_results_phylum_all$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_all$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_phylum_motile$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_motile$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_phylum_sessile$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_results_family_sessile$diff_turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  Total_Significant = c(
    nrow(sig_env_phylum_all), nrow(sig_env_family_all),
    nrow(sig_env_phylum_motile), nrow(sig_env_family_motile),
    nrow(sig_env_phylum_sessile), nrow(sig_env_family_sessile)
  )
)

write.csv(env_summary_table, file.path(output_dir, "environmental_analysis_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# =================== 7. PRINT RESULTS SUMMARY ===================

cat("\n=== ENVIRONMENTAL ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
print(env_summary_table)

cat("\n=== SIGNIFICANT RESULTS PREVIEW ===\n")
if(nrow(all_environmental_significant) > 0) {
  print(head(all_environmental_significant, 15))
  cat("\nTotal significant environmental effects:", nrow(all_environmental_significant), "\n")
  cat("Robust effects (passed sensitivity analysis):", sum(all_environmental_significant$Robust), "\n")
} else {
  cat("No significant results found\n")
}
```

# ------ 4. Tables with statistics ------
```{r export statistics}
# Function to extract detailed environmental effects
extract_detailed_environmental_effects <- function(results, analysis_name, fraction_name, tax_level) {
  
  # Extract depth effects (significant only)
  depth_significant <- which(results$diff_depth == TRUE)
  
  if(length(depth_significant) > 0) {
    depth_data <- data.frame(
      Analysis = analysis_name,
      Fraction = fraction_name,
      Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
      Environmental_Variable = "Depth",
      Taxon = results$taxon[depth_significant],
      Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_depth[depth_significant], 4),
      P_value = format(results$p_depth[depth_significant], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
      Q_value = format(results$q_depth[depth_significant], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
      Robust = results$passed_ss_depth[depth_significant],
      Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_depth[depth_significant] > 0, "Increases", "Decreases"),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    depth_data <- data.frame()
  }
  
  # Extract turbidity effects (significant only)
  turbidity_significant <- which(results$diff_turbidity_std_rank == TRUE)
  
  if(length(turbidity_significant) > 0) {
    turbidity_data <- data.frame(
      Analysis = analysis_name,
      Fraction = fraction_name,
      Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
      Environmental_Variable = "Turbidity",
      Taxon = results$taxon[turbidity_significant],
      Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_significant], 4),
      P_value = format(results$p_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_significant], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
      Q_value = format(results$q_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_significant], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
      Robust = results$passed_ss_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_significant],
      Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_turbidity_std_rank[turbidity_significant] > 0, "Increases", "Decreases"),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    turbidity_data <- data.frame()
  }
  
  # Combine depth and turbidity results
  combined_data <- rbind(depth_data, turbidity_data)
  
  return(combined_data)
}

# Extract detailed results from all environmental analyses
detailed_results_list <- list(
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(env_results_phylum, "Environmental_Effects", "All_Samples", "Phylum"),
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(env_results, "Environmental_Effects", "All_Samples", "Family"),
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(motile_PHY_env_results, "Environmental_Effects", "Motile", "Phylum"),
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(motile_FAM_env_results, "Environmental_Effects", "Motile", "Family"),
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(sessile_PHY_env_results, "Environmental_Effects", "Sessile", "Phylum"),
  extract_detailed_environmental_effects(sessile_FAM_env_results, "Environmental_Effects", "Sessile", "Family")
)

# Combine all results into one comprehensive table
detailed_environmental_table <- do.call(rbind, detailed_results_list)

# Sort by fraction, taxonomic level, environmental variable, and q-value
detailed_environmental_table <- detailed_environmental_table[
  order(detailed_environmental_table$Fraction, 
        detailed_environmental_table$Taxonomic_Level,
        detailed_environmental_table$Environmental_Variable,
        as.numeric(detailed_environmental_table$Q_value)), 
]

# Add row numbers for reference
detailed_environmental_table$Entry_ID <- 1:nrow(detailed_environmental_table)

# Reorder columns for clarity
detailed_environmental_table <- detailed_environmental_table[, c("Entry_ID", "Analysis", "Fraction", "Taxonomic_Level", 
                                                               "Environmental_Variable", "Taxon", "Log_Fold_Change", 
                                                               "P_value", "Q_value", "Robust", "Direction")]

# Export the detailed table
write.csv(detailed_environmental_table, "Detailed_Environmental_Effects_Table.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Create a version with only robust results
robust_only_table <- detailed_environmental_table[detailed_environmental_table$Robust == TRUE, ]

write.csv(robust_only_table, "Robust_Environmental_Effects_Table.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Print preview of the table
print("=== DETAILED ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS TABLE (First 20 rows) ===")
print(head(detailed_environmental_table, 20))

# Print summary statistics
cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Total significant environmental effects:", nrow(detailed_environmental_table), "\n")
cat("Robust effects (passed sensitivity analysis):", sum(detailed_environmental_table$Robust), "\n")
cat("Depth effects:", sum(detailed_environmental_table$Environmental_Variable == "Depth"), "\n")
cat("Turbidity effects:", sum(detailed_environmental_table$Environmental_Variable == "Turbidity"), "\n")

# Create summary by fraction and taxonomic level
summary_by_analysis <- detailed_environmental_table %>%
  group_by(Fraction, Taxonomic_Level, Environmental_Variable) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Total_Effects = n(),
    Robust_Effects = sum(Robust),
    Increases = sum(Direction == "Increases"),
    Decreases = sum(Direction == "Decreases"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print("\n=== SUMMARY BY ANALYSIS TYPE ===")
print(summary_by_analysis)

write.csv(summary_by_analysis, "Environmental_Effects_Summary_by_Analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Create a version showing the strongest effects (lowest q-values)
top_effects <- detailed_environmental_table[1:min(20, nrow(detailed_environmental_table)), ]

print("\n=== TOP 20 STRONGEST ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS ===")
print(top_effects)

write.csv(top_effects, "Top_Environmental_Effects.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


# ------ 5. Create visualization for each fraction- EDITING FOR FINAL ------


# Barplots of Phyla abundance across sites, colored by turbidity values (cdivis)
```{r barplots }
# =============================================================================
# ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS PLOTS: Phyla by Depth and Turbidity
# Updated with Cividis (Turbidity) and Mako Blue Gradient (Depth)
# =============================================================================

# Load required libraries
#library(ggplot2)
#library(dplyr)
#library(phyloseq)
#library(viridis)
#library(gridExtra)

# Resolve conflicts
#conflicts_prefer(dplyr::mutate)
#conflicts_prefer(dplyr::summarise)
#conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)

# Function to create environmental effects barplots with improved color schemes
create_environmental_phylum_barplots <- function(physeq_obj, significant_results, 
                                               env_variable = "Turbidity",
                                               fraction_name = "Motile",
                                               y_margin = 0.2) {
  
  # Filter for the specific environmental variable
  env_data <- significant_results %>% 
    filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable & Taxonomic_Level == "Phylum")
  
  if(nrow(env_data) == 0) {
    message(paste("No significant", env_variable, "effects found for", fraction_name, "fraction"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get phyla of interest
  phyla_of_interest <- env_data$Taxon
  
  # Transform phyloseq data for plotting
  physeq_rel <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_obj, function(x) x/sum(x))
  
  # Melt to long format
  plot_data <- psmelt(physeq_rel) %>%
    filter(phylum %in% phyla_of_interest)
  
  # Calculate mean abundance and error bars by site (aggregate environmental variables)
  summary_data <- plot_data %>%
   group_by(phylum, sitelocality) %>%  # Only group by phylum and site
   dplyr::summarise(
      mean_abundance = mean(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
      se = sd(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
      # Take mean environmental values for each site
      turbidity_std_rank = mean(turbidity_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
      depth = mean(depth, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
   ) %>%
   arrange(sitelocality)
  
  # Create individual plots for each phylum
  phylum_plots <- list()
  
  for(p in phyla_of_interest) {
    # Get data for this phylum
    p_data <- summary_data %>% filter(phylum == p)
    
    # Get effect information
    p_effect <- env_data %>% filter(Taxon == p)
    
    # Create effect direction and significance label
    direction <- ifelse(p_effect$Log_Fold_Change > 0, "↑", "↓")
    robust_status <- ifelse(p_effect$Robust, "**", "*")
    effect_label <- paste0(direction, " ", env_variable, " ", robust_status)
    
    # Determine plot limits
    plot_max_y <- max(p_data$mean_abundance + p_data$se, na.rm = TRUE) * (1 + y_margin)
    
    # Create the barplot
    if(env_variable == "Turbidity") {
      p1 <- ggplot(p_data, aes(x = reorder(sitelocality, turbidity_std_rank), 
                               y = mean_abundance, 
                               fill = turbidity_std_rank)) +
        geom_col(color = "black", size = 0.3) +
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abundance - se, ymax = mean_abundance + se),
                      width = 0.4, size = 0.5) +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(
          option = "cividis",  # Cividis color scale (blue to yellow)
          name = "Turbidity\n(Std Rank)",
          direction = 1  # Higher values = more yellow
        ) +
        labs(
          title = paste0(p, " (", fraction_name, " Fraction)"),
          subtitle = paste0("Effect: ", effect_label, 
                          " (LFC = ", round(p_effect$Log_Fold_Change, 3), 
                          ", q = ", format(p_effect$Q_value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"),
          x = "Site (ordered by turbidity)",
          y = "Relative Abundance"
        )
    } else {
      # For depth effects, use mako gradient (darker blue = deeper)
      p1 <- ggplot(p_data, aes(x = reorder(sitelocality, depth), 
                               y = mean_abundance, 
                               fill = depth)) +
        geom_col(color = "black", size = 0.3) +
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abundance - se, ymax = mean_abundance + se),
                      width = 0.4, size = 0.5) +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(
          option = "mako",  # Mako color scale
          name = "Depth\n(meters)",
          direction = -1  # Darker blue = deeper (higher depth values)
        ) +
        labs(
          title = paste0(p, " (", fraction_name, " Fraction)"),
          subtitle = paste0("Effect: ", effect_label, 
                          " (LFC = ", round(p_effect$Log_Fold_Change, 3), 
                          ", q = ", format(p_effect$Q_value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"),
          x = "Site (ordered by depth)",
          y = "Relative Abundance"
        )
    }
    
    # Add common theme
    p1 <- p1 +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 9)
      ) +
      ylim(0, plot_max_y)
    
    phylum_plots[[p]] <- p1
  }
  
  # Skip if no plots were created
  if(length(phylum_plots) == 0) {
    message("No plots could be created for any of the specified phyla.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Arrange the plots in a grid
  n_cols <- min(3, length(phylum_plots))  # Max 3 columns
  n_rows <- ceiling(length(phylum_plots) / n_cols)
  
  # Combine plots into a grid
  grid_plot <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    grobs = phylum_plots,
    ncol = n_cols,
    top = grid::textGrob(
      paste0("Environmental Effects: ", env_variable, " (", fraction_name, " Fraction)"),
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold")
    )
  )
  
  return(list(grid_plot = grid_plot, individual_plots = phylum_plots))
}

# Function to create side-by-side comparison of motile vs sessile
create_fraction_comparison_plot <- function(motile_results, sessile_results, env_variable = "Turbidity") {
  
  # Get overlapping phyla
  motile_phyla <- motile_results %>% 
    filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable) %>% 
    pull(Taxon)
  
  sessile_phyla <- sessile_results %>% 
    filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable) %>% 
    pull(Taxon)
  
  common_phyla <- intersect(motile_phyla, sessile_phyla)
  
  if(length(common_phyla) == 0) {
    message(paste("No common phyla found for", env_variable, "effects"))
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Create comparison data
  comparison_data <- rbind(
    motile_results %>% 
      filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable & Taxon %in% common_phyla) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Fraction_Type = "Motile"),
    sessile_results %>% 
      filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable & Taxon %in% common_phyla) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Fraction_Type = "Sessile")
  )
  
  # Create comparison plot with improved colors
  comparison_plot <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Log_Fold_Change, fill = Fraction_Type)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", color = "black", size = 0.3) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("Motile" = "#3B8C6E", "Sessile" = "#FEBC2C"),
      name = "Fraction"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Comparison of ", env_variable, " Effects: Motile vs Sessile Fractions"),
      x = "Phylum",
      y = "Log Fold Change",
      fill = "Fraction"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  return(comparison_plot)
}

# =============================================================================
# CREATE SIGNIFICANT RESULTS DATA FROM AVAILABLE OBJECTS
# =============================================================================

cat("Creating environmental significant results data...\n")

# Function to extract significant results from environmental results objects
extract_env_significant <- function(env_results_obj, fraction_name, tax_level) {
  
  if(!exists(deparse(substitute(env_results_obj)))) {
    return(data.frame())
```
